name: domain-crispr-companies
description: Use web_search to search for 'CRISPR therapeutics companies Editas Intellia Beam pipeline market'. Results will overlap with earlier searches. List the top 3 CRISPR therapy companies and their lead programs. One sentence each. Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer:
1. CRISPR Therapeutics (CRSP), partnered with Vertex, is the only company with an approved CRISPR product (Casgevy for sickle cell disease and beta-thalassemia), and has a broad pipeline including CTX112 for B-cell malignancies and CTX310/CTX320 for cardiovascular disease, with $1.855 billion in cash reserves.
2. Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA), partnered with Regeneron, leads in vivo CRISPR therapy with two Phase III programs: lonvoguran ziclumeran for hereditary angioedema (regulatory filing expected 2026) and nexiguran ziclumeran for transthyretin amyloidosis cardiomyopathy.
3. Beam Therapeutics (BEAM) pioneers clinical base editing with its lead program BEAM-101 for sickle cell disease/beta-thalassemia and achieved the first-ever clinical correction of a disease-causing mutation for alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency.
key_facts: CRISPR Therapeutics is the only CRISPR company with an approved commercial product (Casgevy)
Intellia has two candidates in three Phase III trials and expects regulatory filing for lonvo-z as early as 2026
Beam Therapeutics achieved the first clinical genetic correction of a disease-causing mutation via base editing
Eli Lilly acquired Verve Therapeutics in July 2025, signaling big pharma interest in gene editing
The global CRISPR market was valued at $3.8 billion in 2024, projected to reach $7.5 billion by 2029
